person.name: Anudeep Nalla

Outside of tech, Anudeep is a USPA B-licensed skydiver—showcasing his commitment to both adventure and discipline—and balances his engineering life with a passion for fitness and trekking

person.name: Thomas Vaillant
person.bio: I’m a DevOps consultant with 10+ years helping teams ship reliable, cloud‑native software in high‑scale and regulated environments. I started as a full‑stack developer before specializing in DevOps, so I naturally bridge app code, infrastructure, and developer experience. Day to day you’ll find me on Kubernetes, Terraform, CI/CD, and observability; and I’m happy to jump into Go or TypeScript when it helps. I’ve led SRE teams and migrations from legacy to the cloud with a focus on security and pragmatic business impact. I love contributing to open source and leaving teams more autonomous than when I arrived.

person.name: Ye Htet

Beyond coding, I enjoy learning how to deploy and secure systems using Kubernetes, AWS, and Terraform. I'm passionate about the CNCF community and always looking for ways to better connect application development with the infrastructure it runs on.

person.name: Manoj K R
person.bio: I am a DevOps and Cloud Engineer specializing in Kubernetes, container security, and CNCF technologies, with experience managing self-hosted clusters as well as AWS, Azure, and GCP environments. I am an AWS Solutions Architect Associate and Certified Kubernetes Administrator (CKA), actively contributing to Kubernetes upgrades, security patching, and ingress/controller optimizations. I share insights through Meetups and community discussions on CNCF ecosystem tools like cert-manager, Cilium, Linkerd, and Kyverno, helping teams improve reliability and security. You can find more of my work and writings on LinkedIn and my technical blog.

person.name: Rishabh Jain
person.bio: Rishabh is a Senior Cloud Support Engineer at Amazon Web Services (AWS), specializing in Amazon EC2 and Linux and Container services. As a subject matter expert, he brings extensive experience in managing enterprise-scale cloud infrastructure and security implementations. His expertise encompasses container orchestration platforms including Kubernetes, Amazon ECS, and Amazon EKS. Rishabh plays a pivotal role in guiding organizations through cloud migrations and modernization initiatives, ensuring the implementation of industry best practices for cloud-based workloads. As a certified public speaker and key member of the Containers focus area, he continues to contribute to the cloud computing community through knowledge sharing and technical leadership.

person.name: Wolfram Gerret Apelt
person.bio: Gerret is a technology enthusiast with over 20 years of experience in IT operations consulting. He got into programming as a teenager, built his first OpenStack private cloud in 2015 and thinks of documentation as an art-form. He thrives in architectural roles that require vision and challenge his deep technical expertise. He is employed as a Senior Operations Engineer with IS Nordic A/S where he designs, implements and operates strong Kubernetes clusters both on-premise and in the cloud, for clients as well as as in-house workloads.

person.name: Gusty Sapto Ady Prakoso

With experience in the financial services and technology industries, I focus on building resilient and secure infrastructure using CNCF technologies like Kubernetes, Terraform, Argo, Falco, Cilium, OPA, Threatmapper, Trivy, OpenTelemetry, LGTM Stack, etc.I actively contribute to internal tech sharing sessions in my company to promote DevSecOps culture and cloud-native best practices.You can find more about me at https://gusty.id.

person.name: Junya Okabe
person.bio: He is passionate about cloud native technologies, especially Kubernetes, and is a professional in this field. He leads the localization of Kubernetes and CNCF documentation as a localization approver in SIG-Docs and a reviewer for several projects. Additionally, he is an active OSS contributor to Kubernetes and related projects. To encourage more people to join the community, he is part of the organizer team for Kubernetes Upstream Training. (Related blog post: https://kubernetes.io/blog/2024/10/28/k8s-upstream-training-japan-spotlight/ )

person.name: Máté Eckl

person.name: Seoyul Yoon
person.bio: Hi, I’m Seoyul Yoon. I’m a Cloud Native Application Engineer focused on architecting and building Kubernetes-based AI platforms. I actively contribute to the broader Cloud Native Computing Foundation ecosystem and serve as an organizer of Cloud Native Community Korea (CNCK), where I help drive community initiatives and technical knowledge sharing.  I believe cloud native technologies will fundamentally transform how systems are built and operated. Recently, I’ve been expanding my focus into Cloud Native AI (CNAI), exploring how AI-native workloads can be operationalized on Kubernetes.  If you’re interested, please check the link below: CNCK: https://community.cncf.io/cloud-native-community-korea/

person.name: Tianyi Yang
person.bio: I am a seasoned full-stack software engineer with over 17 years of experience in developing and architecting web framework platform solutions across diverse industries. Specializing in Kubernetes and CNCF technologies, I have led significant projects, focusing on cloud system testing and monitoring platforms using Kubernetes, Helm, and Terraform. I am a Kubestronaut and highly proficient in system design, automation, and DevOps practices.

person.name: Uroš Hercog
person.bio: I'm a Site Reliability Engineer at Veza, the Identity Security company, primarily focusing on tools like Kubernetes, Prometheus, and Cilium. My expertise lies in optimizing infrastructure resilience and automating deployment processes to improve reliability across dynamic environments.

person.name: Audra Montenegro
person.bio: Over the past eleven years, Audra has evolved from organizing global events, to empowering others to do the same, to becoming a speaker herself. For seven of those years, she led programming for both major tech conferences and intimate summits at O’Reilly Media.Audra’s passion for community building and her expertise in event management have made her a sought-after speaker at conferences such as KubeCon + CloudNativeCon, DevOpsDays, and DevOps World. She has also been a featured speaker at the Open Source Summit, where she shared her insights on the intersection of open source and community engagement.Since 2022, Audra has served as a Community and Outreach Manager at the Cloud Native Computing Foundation (CNCF), where she drives global initiatives like Kubernetes Community Days (KCDs), Cloud Native Community Groups (CNCGs), the ChatLoopBackOff (CLBO) livestream series, and CNCF’s member video and streaming programs.Audra is fueled by a commitment to elevate emerging voices and deepen community engagement. Her passion lies in connecting industry leaders, spotlighting technical innovators, and creating platforms where ideas spark, grow, and resonate globally.

person.name: Itay Talmi
person.bio: I’m a Kubernetes expert with extensive experience designing, automating, and scaling cloud-native environments. I focus on building practical demos and real-world solutions that showcase advanced Kubernetes capabilities such as GitOps, autoscaling, Crossplane integration, Tekton pipelines, and HashiCorp Vault deployments, and many many more. I’m passionate about helping organizations adopt Kubernetes efficiently and cost-effectively, combining deep technical expertise with a focus on simplicity, automation, and improving the developer experience. My blog: https://cloudnativeapps.blog

person.name: Ivo Grondman

I’m now looking to shift my focus towards platform engineering, infrastructure tooling and observability.

For deeper insights into my projects and perspectives on innovation

I love the thrill of certification exams and constantly challenging myself to go deeper into the CNCF ecosystem. You can find my writings and technical adventures on my blog at yasincaykur.com.
